message.content:
{
  "lecture_id": "optimisation_001",
  "knowledge_objects": [
    {
      "id": "objective_function",
      "name": "Objective Function",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A function that assigns a real value to each feasible input in an optimisation problem.",
      "source_span": "an objective function assigns a real value to each feasible input"
    },
    {
      "id": "optimisation_problem",
      "name": "Optimisation Problem",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A problem that asks for an input that makes the objective function as small or as large as possible.",
      "source_span": "An optimisation problem asks for an input that makes the objective function as small or as large as possible"
    },
    {
      "id": "unconstrained_minimisation_problem",
      "name": "Unconstrained Minimisation Problem",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "An optimisation problem where the feasible inputs are all points in a space and the goal is to minimise a differentiable function.",
      "source_span": "unconstrained minimisation problem"
    },
    {
      "id": "gradient",
      "name": "Gradient",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A vector that describes local change of a differentiable function.",
      "source_span": "gradient \\(\\nabla f(x)\\) describes local change"
    },
    {
      "id": "descent_direction",
      "name": "Descent Direction",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A direction that points toward the direction of steepest local decrease, given by the negative gradient.",
      "source_span": "negative gradient \\(-\\nabla f(x)\\) is a descent direction"
    },
    {
      "id": "gradient_descent",
      "name": "Gradient Descent",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A first-order iterative method that repeatedly moves in the descent direction to minimise a function.",
      "source_span": "Gradient descent is a first-order iterative method that repeatedly moves in this direction"
    },
    {
      "id": "step_size",
      "name": "Step Size",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A scalar that controls how far the method moves at each iteration.",
      "source_span": "scalar \\(\\alpha_k\\) is the step size"
    },
    {
      "id": "line_search",
      "name": "Line Search",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A method for choosing a step size that gives sufficient decrease in the objective function.",
      "source_span": "A line search is a method for choosing a step size that gives sufficient decrease in the objective function"
    },
    {
      "id": "convex_function",
      "name": "Convex Function",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A function for which every local minimum is also a global minimum.",
      "source_span": "convex functions"
    },
    {
      "id": "stationary_point",
      "name": "Stationary Point",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A point where the gradient is zero, describing candidates for local optima.",
      "source_span": "stationary point is a point where the gradient is zero"
    },
    {
      "id": "gradient_descent_update",
      "name": "Gradient Descent Update",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The update rule for gradient descent: x_{k+1} = x_k - alpha_k * gradient f(x_k).",
      "source_span": "\\[ x_{k+1} = x_k - \\alpha_k \\nabla f(x_k). \\]"
    }
  ]
}
